ogTitle: Stirling Range National Park vs Stoneville: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Stirling Range National Park and Stoneville.
heading: Stirling Range National Park vs Stoneville — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Stirling Range National Park, Western Australia and Stoneville, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Stirling Range National Park has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Stoneville (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Stirling Range National Park or Stoneville safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Stirling Range National Park has a flood risk score of 36/100, while Stoneville scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Stirling Range National Park scores 34/100 for bushfire risk. Stoneville scores 35/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Stirling Range National Park or Stoneville?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Stirling Range National Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
